message:
fix(indexer): correct watch-state window logic and add semantic write safety

Indexer watch-state / reindex_paths:

- Fix the scan-window start timestamp selection in reindex_paths so that
  when both a previous watermark and a batch min_ts exist, the *older*
  of the two is used.  Previously the code unconditionally subtracted 1
  from the previous watermark even when the batch trigger timestamp was
  much older, causing out-of-order file events to be silently skipped
  during incremental re-indexing.

- Only advance the per-connector watch-state watermark when at least one
  conversation was actually indexed (conv_count > 0).  Before this fix,
  a scan that yielded zero conversations (e.g. malformed file, transient
  parse failure) would still ratchet the watermark forward to the file's
  max_ts, permanently skipping those files on subsequent watch cycles.

- Add two new unit tests:
  - reindex_paths_uses_oldest_trigger_window_when_state_is_newer:
    verifies that when the stored watermark is far in the future relative
    to the trigger file's timestamp, indexing still picks up the file.
  - reindex_paths_does_not_advance_watch_state_when_scan_yields_no_conversations:
    verifies that an intentionally malformed file leaves the watermark
    unchanged so the file will be retried on the next cycle.

Semantic indexer (semantic.rs):

- Wrap the vector-index write loop in a closure so that on any write
  failure (dimension mismatch, fsvi write error) the partial index file
  is cleaned up via fs::remove_file before returning the error.  This
  prevents corrupted partial vector index files from persisting on disk
  and being loaded on the next startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
